$comment: SOURCE OF TRUTH for the Repo & process contract. ONE contract holds secrets, Python toolchain, imports, file naming, and commit conventions as rules. Dependencies is a SEPARATE contract (governance/allowed-deps.json). chattr backend is Python (uv); frontend is built to the v3 standard.
title: Repo & process
summary: Repo hygiene and process: secrets via SOPS only (no plaintext env), the Python toolchain (uv), import/path conventions, file & directory naming, and commit conventions. The dependency allowlist is its own contract — see Dependencies.

rules.text: Secrets go through SOPS only. No plaintext `.env` files anywhere, under any name. A `.gitignore` is NOT protection. Docs reference SOPS pointers + commands, never raw values.

`uv` is the only Python package manager/toolchain for the backend. No repo-local `.venv` (cross-drive hardlink failures) — `UV_PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT` points uv at an environment outside the checkout. Python dependencies are declared in `pyproject.toml` and allowlisted in `governance/allowed-deps.json`.

enforcedBy: scripted scan + dependency-cruiser — derived from these rules
enforcement: Secrets scan — flags any plaintext secret / `.env` file committed to the repo.
openItems: Imports & path conventions — define and lock.
File & directory naming — define and lock.
